sections.heading: 1. Introduction
content: This document outlines the tax policies, compliance requirements, and financial regulations applicable to services provided by Adymire Agency ("the Company," "We," or "Us").
This information is designed to help clients understand:
• Applicable taxes on services
• Tax compliance obligations
• Invoicing and documentation requirements
• International tax considerations
• Regulatory compliance
By engaging our services, you acknowledge and accept these tax policies and agree to comply with all applicable tax laws and regulations.

subSections.heading: 2.1 Company Registration Details
content: • Legal Name: Adymire Agency
• Registered Address: [Your Business Address]
• Business Type: [Sole Proprietorship / Partnership / Private Limited / LLP]

subSections.heading: 2.2 Tax Registrations
content: GOODS AND SERVICES TAX (GST):
• GST Registration Number (GSTIN): [Your GSTIN]
• State of Registration: [Your State]
• GST Status: Regular / Composite (as applicable)
PAN (PERMANENT ACCOUNT NUMBER):
• PAN: [Your PAN]
OTHER REGISTRATIONS:
• TAN (Tax Deduction Account Number): [Your TAN]
• Import Export Code (IEC): [If applicable]
• MSME Registration: [If applicable]

subSections.heading: 3.1 Goods And Services Tax (Gst) - India
content: For clients located in India:
CGST + SGST (Intra-State Supply):
• Condition: When Company and Client are in the same state
• CGST (Central GST): [Current rate - typically 9%]
• SGST (State GST): [Current rate - typically 9%]
• Total: 18% (or as per current GST rates)
IGST (Inter-State Supply):
• Condition: When Company and Client are in different states
• IGST (Integrated GST): 18% (or as per current GST rates)
GST RATES BY SERVICE (HSN/SAC):
• Software Development: SAC 998314 - 18% GST
• Digital Marketing: SAC 998399 - 18% GST
• Design Services: SAC 998399 - 18% GST
• Consulting Services: SAC 998212 - 18% GST
• Web Hosting: SAC 997212 - 18% GST
Note: GST rates are subject to change based on government notifications.

subSections.heading: 3.2 Tax Deducted At Source (Tds)
content: Certain clients may be required to deduct TDS on payments under the following sections:
Section 194J (Professional or Technical Services):
• TDS Rate: 10% (for companies) or 10% (for individuals/HUF)
• Threshold: Applicable when payment exceeds ₹30,000 in a financial year
Section 194C (Contractor Payments):
• TDS Rate: 1-2% (depending on payer category)
WHO MUST DEDUCT:
• Companies, firms, and businesses making specified payments
• Individual professionals with turnover above specified limits
• Government entities
COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS:
• Inform Company in advance of deduction
• Provide TDS certificate (Form 16A) within prescribed timeline
• Ensure TDS reflects in Company's Form 26AS

subSections.heading: 3.3 International Clients (Export Of Services)
content: ZERO-RATED SUPPLY:
• Services exported are generally 0% GST
• Conditions: Payment in convertible foreign exchange; Recipient outside India; Meets export criteria
DOCUMENTATION REQUIRED:
• Valid international address
• Payment via wire transfer, PayPal, etc.
• Export invoice with specific declaration
WITHHOLDING TAX:
• Clients responsible for local tax law compliance
• Tax treaties (DTAA) between India and client's country may apply

subSections.heading: 4.1 Invoice Components
content: All invoices issued by the Company will include the following mandatory and specific details:
MANDATORY DETAILS:
• Invoice number (unique sequential number)
• Invoice date
• Company name, address, and GSTIN
• Client name, address, and GSTIN (if applicable)
• Description of services rendered
• Quantity and rate of service
• Taxable value (base amount before tax)
• GST rate and amount (split as CGST/SGST or IGST)
• Total invoice value (including taxes)
• Payment terms and due date
• Bank details for payment remittance
FOR INTERNATIONAL CLIENTS:
• Mandatory mention: 'Export of Services - Zero Rated Supply'
• Transaction Currency (typically USD, EUR, or as agreed)
• Conversion rate applied (if applicable)

subSections.heading: 4.2 Tax Invoice Vs. Bill Of Supply
content: The type of document issued depends on the taxability of the transaction:
TAX INVOICE:
• Issued when GST is applicable (Domestic supplies)
• Contains full breakdown of GST components
BILL OF SUPPLY:
• Issued for zero-rated exports
• Does not contain GST charge details

subSections.heading: 4.3 Credit Notes And Debit Notes
content: In case of value adjustments post-invoicing:
CREDIT NOTE:
• Issued when invoice amount needs to be reduced (e.g., refunds, cancellations, or discounts)
• Effectively reverses the original GST charged
DEBIT NOTE:
• Issued when invoice amount needs to be increased (e.g., additional charges or scope changes)
• Adds to the original GST liability

subSections.heading: 4.4 Invoice Delivery
content: • Invoices will be sent via email to the designated billing contact provided by the client
• Digital invoices are legally valid documents under GST law
• Physical copies can be provided upon specific request

subSections.heading: 5.1 Providing Tax Information
content: Clients must provide accurate data to ensure legal compliance:
FOR INDIAN CLIENTS:
• Legal business name as per registration
• Complete billing address including PIN code
• GSTIN (if registered under GST)
• PAN (if applicable)
• Nature of business
FOR INTERNATIONAL CLIENTS:
• Legal business name
• Complete address with country name
• Local Tax identification number (if applicable)
• Preferred currency for invoicing

subSections.heading: 5.2 Gst Registration
content: • If your business turnover exceeds ₹20 lakhs (₹10 lakhs for special category states), you must register for GST
• Voluntary GST registration is also permitted
• Note: Unregistered businesses will still be charged GST but will be unable to claim input tax credit

subSections.heading: 5.3 Input Tax Credit (Itc)
content: For GST-registered businesses wishing to claim credit:
• You can claim input tax credit on GST paid to the Company
• Requirement: Ensure the invoice is in your business name with the correct GSTIN
• Compliance: File your returns timely to claim ITC
• Verification: The Company will upload invoices to GSTN; ensure they reflect in your GSTR-2A/2B

subSections.heading: 5.4 Record Keeping
content: Clients should maintain documentation as follows:
• Maintain copies of all invoices and payment receipts
• Keep records for at least 6 years as per tax regulations
• Ensure accurate accounting for future tax filing or audits

subSections.heading: 6.1 Payment Inclusive Of Tax
content: • All quoted prices are exclusive of taxes unless explicitly stated otherwise in the contract
• GST will be added as a separate line item to the base service charge
• For international clients: Prices are typically exclusive of any local taxes or withholding requirements in your country

subSections.heading: 6.2 Tds Deductions
content: If you are legally required to deduct TDS:
• Inform the Company before making the actual payment
• Deduct the applicable TDS amount and deposit it with the government
• Provide the TDS certificate (Form 16A) within 15 days of filing your TDS return
• Pay the net balance amount to the Company after the TDS deduction
EXAMPLE:
• Invoice Base Amount: ₹100,000
• Total Invoice Value: ₹118,000
• Final Amount Payable to Company: ₹108,000

subSections.heading: 6.3 Tax Compliance Failures
content: If the client fails to deduct TDS, provide certificates, or provide correct tax info:
THE COMPANY MAY:
• Adjust future invoices to account for outstanding tax liabilities
• Suspend ongoing services until full compliance is achieved
• Report non-compliance to tax authorities if legally required by statute

subSections.heading: 7.1 Export Of Services Criteria
content: For services to qualify as export:
PLACE OF SUPPLY:
• Recipient must be located outside India
• Services must be consumed/utilized outside India
PAYMENT:
• Must be received in convertible foreign exchange
• Or in Indian Rupees if permitted by RBI
DOCUMENTATION:
• Export invoice mentioning zero-rated supply
• Payment confirmation showing foreign exchange receipt
• Service delivery evidence (if required)

subSections.heading: 7.2 Foreign Exchange Compliance
content: Payments from international clients must comply with FEMA (Foreign Exchange Management Act) regulations:
bullets: Payments from international clients must comply with FEMA (Foreign Exchange Management Act) regulations
Company is required to report foreign exchange receipts to banks and authorities
Delayed or non-receipt of foreign exchange may attract regulatory scrutiny

subSections.heading: 7.3 Transfer Pricing
content: For related party transactions or significant international dealings:
• Transfer pricing regulations may apply
• Arm's length pricing principles must be followed
• Company may be required to maintain transfer pricing documentation

subSections.heading: 8.1 Company Obligations
content: The Company is responsible for:
GST COMPLIANCE:
• File monthly/quarterly GST returns (GSTR-1, GSTR-3B)
• Pay GST to the government
• Upload supplier invoices to GSTN
• Maintain proper books of accounts
INCOME TAX COMPLIANCE:
• File annual income tax returns
• Pay advance tax and final tax liabilities
• Maintain financial statements
TDS COMPLIANCE (IF APPLICABLE):
• Deduct TDS on specified payments
• Deposit TDS with the government
• Issue TDS certificates
• File TDS returns

subSections.heading: 8.2 Client Obligations
content: Clients are responsible for:
• Providing accurate tax information
• Deducting TDS when applicable and providing certificates
• Complying with their own tax filing requirements
• Claiming input tax credit appropriately
• Maintaining records as per law

subSections.heading: 8.3 Regulatory Reporting
content: The Company may be required to report certain transactions to:
• GST authorities
• Income Tax Department
• RBI (for foreign exchange transactions)
• Other regulatory bodies
Client information will be shared only as required by law.

subSections.heading: 9.1 Company Tax Audits
content: If the Company undergoes tax audits
• Client information may be requested by authorities
• Company will maintain confidentiality to the extent legally possible
• Clients may be contacted by authorities for verification (rare)

subSections.heading: 9.2 Client Tax Audits
content: If clients undergo tax audits
• Company will provide necessary invoices and documentation
• Company will cooperate with reasonable information requests
• Client is responsible for their own tax position and representation

subSections.heading: 9.3 Tax Disputes
content: • In case of tax disputes related to services
• Both parties will cooperate in good faith
• Company will provide necessary documentation
• Each party is responsible for their own tax liabilities

subSections.heading: 10.1 Reverse Charge Mechanism (Rcm)
content: In certain cases, GST is payable by the recipient (reverse charge):
• Typically does not apply to our services
• If applicable, will be mentioned on the invoice
• Client will be responsible for paying GST directly to the government

subSections.heading: 10.2 E-Commerce Operators
content: If services are booked through an e-commerce platform:
• The platform may be required to collect and pay GST (TCS - Tax Collected at Source)
• This is separate from the service GST
• Applicable if platform turnover exceeds specified limits

subSections.heading: 10.3 Government And Psu Clients
content: • May have different TDS rates or exemptions
• Required documentation may vary
• Payment processes may be subject to government procedures

subSections.heading: 11.1 Adaptability
content: • Tax rates, regulations, and compliance requirements may change
• The Company will adapt invoicing and compliance as per updated laws
• Clients will be notified of significant changes affecting them

subSections.heading: 11.2 Retroactive Changes
content: • In rare cases, tax laws may be applied retroactively
• Both parties will cooperate to adjust for such changes
• Historical invoices may need to be revised if legally required

subSections.heading: 12.1 Company Is Not A Tax Advisor
content: • This document provides general tax information
• It is not a substitute for professional tax advice
• Clients should consult their own tax advisors for specific situations

subSections.heading: 12.2 No Liability For Client's Tax Obligations
content: The Company is not liable for:
• Client's failure to comply with tax laws
• Incorrect tax information provided by the client
• Client's inability to claim input tax credit
• Penalties or interest arising from client's tax non-compliance

subSections.heading: 12.3 Accuracy Of Information
content: • Information is provided in good faith based on current knowledge
• Company is not liable for errors or omissions in tax information
• Clients should verify tax details independently

sections.heading: 13. Amendments To Tax Policies
content: The Company reserves the right to update this document based on:
• Changes in tax laws and regulations
• Business requirement changes
• Regulatory guidance and clarifications
Updated versions will be posted on our website. Continued use of services constitutes acceptance of updated policies.

sections.heading: 14. Record Retention
content: As per tax laws:
COMPANY WILL RETAIN:
• Invoices and financial records for at least 6 years
• Tax returns and supporting documents as per law
• Payment and transaction records
CLIENTS SHOULD RETAIN:
• All invoices and receipts
• Payment confirmations
• TDS certificates
• Correspondence related to tax matters

sections.heading: 15. Contact For Tax Queries
content: For questions related to tax matters, invoicing, or compliance:
Adymire Agency
Accounts Department
• Email: [Your Accounts Email]
• Phone: [Your Official Phone Number]
• Address: [Your Registered Business Address]
For tax-specific documentation requests:
• Email with clear details of requirement
• Allow 5-7 business days for processing
• Urgent requests may be accommodated subject to availability

sections.heading: 16. Acceptance And Acknowledgment
content: By engaging our services, you acknowledge that:
• You have read and understood this Tax Information & Compliances document
• You agree to comply with all applicable tax laws and regulations
• You will provide accurate tax information as required
• You understand your tax obligations related to our services
• You will cooperate with reasonable tax compliance requests
If you have any questions or concerns about tax matters, please contact us before proceeding with service engagement.
